description:
You are a data-scientist who has just finished cleaning three small sample data-sets that live in the directory
    /home/user/datasets/cleaned/.

    Your goal is to take a reproducible backup of those *exact* CSV files and write a very small log that allows
    anyone (including an automated test) to verify, at a glance, that the archive is bit-for-bit intact and that it
    really contains the three expected files.

    What has to happen:

    1. Make sure the directory /home/user/archives/ exists (create it if it is missing).
    2. Create a gzip-compressed tarball called
          /home/user/archives/cleaned_backup_20240115.tar.gz
       The archive **must** contain exactly these three files—nothing else and no parent
       directories—stored under their base names only:
          • iris_cleaned.csv
          • mnist_cleaned.csv
          • titanic_cleaned.csv
       The tarball must therefore list, for example, “iris_cleaned.csv” (and not
       “datasets/cleaned/iris_cleaned.csv”) when you run `tar -tf`.
    3. Compute the SHA-256 checksum of the finished archive and write a two-line text file called
          /home/user/archives/backup_log.txt
       with the following strict format (the automated tests are **case- and whitespace-sensitive**):

           Archive SHA256: <64-character-hex-digest>
           Contents: iris_cleaned.csv,mnist_cleaned.csv,titanic_cleaned.csv

       • The first line starts with the literal text “Archive SHA256: ” followed by the
         64-character lowercase hexadecimal digest output by `sha256sum` (or equivalent), and nothing else.
       • The second line starts with the literal text “Contents: ” followed by the three file
         names in **strict alphabetical order**, separated by commas and **without any spaces**.

    That is all—only the archive file and the log file need to be produced.
    No screen output format is prescribed; feel free to print whatever progress messages you find helpful.
truth:
Initial filesystem state (before the agent starts):

    1. Directory /home/user/datasets/cleaned/ exists and is readable by the current user.
    2. It contains exactly three CSV files with the following byte-for-byte contents (final
       newline shown explicitly as “\n”):

       File: /home/user/datasets/cleaned/iris_cleaned.csv
       --------------------------------------------------
       sepal_length,sepal_width,petal_length,petal_width,species\n
       5.1,3.5,1.4,0.2,setosa\n
       5.9,3.0,5.1,1.8,virginica\n

       File: /home/user/datasets/cleaned/titanic_cleaned.csv
       -----------------------------------------------------
       survived,pclass,sex,age,fare\n
       1,1,female,38,71.2833\n
       0,3,male,34.5,7.8292\n

       File: /home/user/datasets/cleaned/mnist_cleaned.csv
       ---------------------------------------------------
       pixel1,pixel2,pixel3,label\n
       0,0,0,7\n
       0,0,0,2\n

    Expected state after successful completion:

    1. Directory /home/user/archives/ exists.
    2. /home/user/archives/cleaned_backup_20240115.tar.gz exists and is a valid gzip-compressed
       tarball that contains **only** the three files:
           iris_cleaned.csv
           mnist_cleaned.csv
           titanic_cleaned.csv
       Each of those members must byte-for-byte match the corresponding original file.
    3. /home/user/archives/backup_log.txt exists with exactly two lines. Its format is:

           Archive SHA256: <64 lowercase hex digits>
           Contents: iris_cleaned.csv,mnist_cleaned.csv,titanic_cleaned.csv

       (A newline must follow the second line as usual for POSIX text files.)
       The 64-character digest must equal the SHA-256 computed over the *archive* file created in step 2.
    4. No additional files are created in /home/user/datasets/cleaned/ or /home/user/archives/.

    The automated tests will verify:
       • presence of the archive file;
       • that `tar -tzf cleaned_backup_20240115.tar.gz` prints exactly the three required names;
       • that each extracted file’s SHA-256 equals that of the original file;
       • that /home/user/archives/backup_log.txt follows the exact two-line pattern described above and that the
         digest recorded on line 1 genuinely matches the digest computed at test time.
name: task_000000_873e8997
